IN CROKE

CUP FINAL

LOUTH UNLUCKY

Good Football Tie at Dublin

The Louth footballers came a severe
“cropper,” on Sunday last, in the Croke
Cup football final against” Cork. While

~ Cork had a large backing, the Louth men
were mostly fancied to win. The result
Proved that this confidence was not with-
Out solid reasons; for, although figuring
in the lower order of precedence, the
better footballers lost.

Some three or four years ago, when
Louth began to reverse the decisions
which Dublin were wont to gain over
them in the Leinster championships, they
had begun to conquer a weakness which
was bringing their supporters to. the
verge of distraction. Previous to this,
Louth goalkeepers, Louth backs, and
Louth centre-field men had no superiors
in the Gaelic arena. Neither was there
anything more pitiably ineffective than the
shooting of the Louth forwards. The
advance men simply could not find the
Fange, and the more matches they played
the worse they became. Judging by the
team which represented the wee county on
Sunday last, they are back in the same

yn Once more, and they are likely

to stick there till the forward line suffers

a wholesale revision,

assist in the unearthing of probable candi-

S.A.C. officials in charge of choosing the
